transcript.text: Hey, Tyler, how's it going?
Pretty good, pretty good.
Getting close to the end of the long-term Monday and watching way too much football on the weekends.
Yeah, who's your team?
Texas A&M.
So we had a big win versus Notre Dame, and we almost always lose those type of games.
Nice.
Yeah, for me, NFL Sunday is more important than College Football Saturday, but it's been an exciting year.
I feel like a lot of really good matchups in College Football early on.
Absolutely.
I guess y'all do have like one huge rivalry game, right, Yale, at the end of the year?
Is it Harvard or is it?
Yeah, we have the Harvard-Yale game.
Okay.
Yeah, that's fun, but other than that, like, no one really cares about football.
That's the one weekend where everybody does care, though, right?
Everyone, yeah, tries.
So I need to learn the rules of the sport.
Yeah.
Who's our quarterback again?
What's his name?
Cool, man.
Well, I figured today probably won't be too long or difficult, just kind of going over the partnership agreement and making sure we answer any questions you guys had.
I think when it comes to the team, just like let us know what you guys are trying to optimize for.
And I'll be able to get the team assigned, like once we've got the date, you know, alignment to move forward.
It's just kind of difficult because I like don't know what the ask is, right?
To be able to like proactively assign a team that I don't exactly know what you're hoping to optimize for.
So, I mean, yeah, we can start there.
you have more context on, I guess, what you guys are hoping for?
Or what Stu wants?
Yeah, I think we're, the end goal is to, is to like have SEO be a channel that's actually driving meetings for us.
And I think for us, we're pretty focused right now as are other companies just on like AEO and optimizing that, how we can improve our ranking and coverage with NLLMs.

So I think that would be like the main strategy sprint would be going into is like identifying what queries are coming up a lot, how we position ourselves and make sure that we can start to get suggested by these AI answer engines.
But yeah, I think our primary focus would be more on the end of the new content and for updating everything that we have already.
think we would probably just like the attention team would handle that ourselves.
And And And And And
More on, yeah, net new content versus refreshes.
But yeah, I mean, I'm like not coming from a background in SEO.

And I think that's maybe where the strategy sprint would be helpful, like could be helpful in terms of like working with your team to also for you to do an analysis of our positioning and like what are the actual opportunities that we need to uncover, right?
Does that make sense?
Yeah, that makes perfect sense.
I guess I just didn't know if like the last time y'all partnered with us, there was an issue with like one of the team members that was working with Attention in particular.
And if I knew more about that, then I could, you know, like tell the team, not like, you know, hey, so-and-so didn't do a good job.
More so just like, hey, we were lacking in XYZ area and like Attention wants to make sure that the team that's assigned isn't going to have that same problem.
I came here and I think when I got to GrowthX, was here after you guys had already turned.
good good We'll
No, I don't have context on that either.
I don't think that there are any like specific.
Yeah, I don't think that ask wasn't like because we had issues with someone specific on the team.
It's just more like, I think we like to know who we would be working with.
So, yeah, I think.
I see.
It's just more like, hey, like who's going to be on our account so that we can just check out their background and I don't know, just know what we're getting ourselves into.
Got it.
I thought it was more of a, hey, like last time we worked with you guys, there was someone on our account.
They just didn't do the best job.
Can we make sure that we get a different person?
So that, that helps.
Like we don't have to necessarily like, you know, make sure that there are certain people who are not staffed for attention as a client.
It's more so just communicating who you're working with.
Got it.
Okay, cool.
Well, I guess anything else that you want to cover besides just.
Reviewing the agreement together and talking through the final steps.
Yeah, I mean, if you'd be, yeah, I looked at the scope of work, seems like relatively straightforward.
Is there anything in particular that is worth you, like, worth having you do a voiceover on top of?
Yeah, for sure.
Let me bring your note taker.
I just saw it in the waiting room.
Let me bring that in as well so I can have this recorded for Stu.
Stu?
Perfect.
So, the SoW, I know that, you know, we talked about how you guys were looking to move a little bit faster.
And so, I think, I talked to Marcel, the simplest thing we should do is just keep our strategy sprint as is.
Because if we moved it to four weeks, like, we don't go lower than 20 on the strategy sprint.
So, what we can do is just keep this at eight weeks at the 20, but we'll get to...
Publishing much faster, since that's the focus, right?
You guys want to publish new content.
We've worked together before, so we have context.
We're just going to start doing what we would do in growth execution earlier in the engagement when we finish up the artifacts, basically.
So this will be the term length for phase one, phase two.
We've got the 12K approved, even though we do 15 for any new clients, since you guys had the legacy pricing.
And our earliest available kickoff is the 27th right now.
And I can reserve that for y'all, just once you give me a thumbs up on when we could expect signature and your desire to move forward.
So those are the big pieces when it comes to the agreement.
Happy to answer any questions, though.
Hey, Stu.
Sorry, I just saw you.
Okay, so to recap, it's 20K for the next two months.
So it's effectively 10K a month for the strategy.
That's right.
And then it's 12 months with 12K per month for like the recurring agreement after that.
That's right.
Okay.
And so the strategy sprint starts on the 27th and our 12K then doesn't kick in until roughly December.
Exactly.
Basically, end of the year would be when the phase two first semiannual.
We would have some content start to get published and produced like starting with during the strategy sprint period.
Absolutely.
For most clients, we're publishing by week four, week five.
For you guys, ideally, we could even publish sooner since we've created content before.
So you would be able to like make that opt-out decision on phase two.
five, five, five, five, week with

With several articles that you can look at for reference and a good feeling about what the work would be as we continue into the growth execution and how you feel about, you know, the team that you've been set up with.
Got it.
Okay.
Um, yeah, and Tyler was saying if like we had specific members of the team that we wanted to work with, we could request them and try to make it work.
Um, but it's hard to totally project out.
Yeah.
Or like if there was someone you did not have a good experience with, that was going to be one thing I could just factor in to make sure you're not going to work with that person.
Um, there's this guy, Eric, who's been the lead on a lot of our strategy sprints.
And then you guys would, um, have a team of like a managing director, a managing editor, and a content manager in phase two.
Um, Marcel typically joins like the kickoff and the strategy sprint as well.
See See
You'd have him there.
And then there's our chief of staff, George, who is very involved in the strategy sprint.
I just sent you Eric's LinkedIn, who does a lot of the strategy sprint work.
He came from like Slalom Consulting and has been like head of client services and delivery at the last organization he was at, which was more AI forward.
Cool.
Yeah, I'd say like we don't really have enough context.
On your team to figure out like who we want to work with, but I think just understanding like who we would be working with is helpful.
Does this contract outline any sort of expectation on deliverables?
I see like the strategy sprint.
We have like some of that stuff.
We have the content volume five pieces per week.
Okay, content volume is five pieces per week.
Okay, got it.
Yeah, and I told Harrison that we could like make sure those are all net new if that was your preference.
It sounds like that's the preference.
So we just won't do any refresh as part of the five.
All the five each week will be net new.
Is that going to happen during the strategy sprint or it's only during the growth execution phase?
We'll publish during the strategy sprint, but every client's different.
So we don't connect a volume to phase one.
What I've seen is that you'll have at least 10 articles that have been published.
So we'd start publishing in like week five, week four.
And then, you know, the first couple articles are calibration articles, which require a lot of input.
We want to make sure like we feel really good about the quality before we're scaling it out.
But 10 is probably a good number to think about in terms of having, you know, published on your CMS, being able to look at those in terms of...
In of opting out of phase two or continuing on, that'd probably be the most clarity I could give you there.
Got it.
Okay, cool.
Yeah, I mean, initial reaction to the contract is like, it's extremely favorable to you guys.
It's like eight weeks, right?
And then it's another 12 months on top of that.
Yeah.
And then it's billed semi-annually up front.
Like, that's a pretty big ask, I think, relative to just month-to-month payments.
I think the biggest thing still right now is just like the idea of a 12-month commitment with this type of service.
Like, I view this more as services or like contracting in a way.
But I think normally, like, whether it's a web designer or a contractor, advisor, like, we'll normally do like three months.
Month commitments at the most.
And I think for us, right, like when we're buying services and not a platform, like we want to make sure people are motivated month to month and quarter to quarter to deliver on things.
Whereas like, I don't know, a little afraid, like we hop into a 12 month, like by month seven, right?
Like is the hunger still there to kind of like grind for us, right?
Um, yeah.
So I think that's like a neutral reaction.
Especially because this is almost the cost of like an annual SEO employee in house, right?
I mean, it pretty much is.
So you can think of like, we're evaluating growthx versus bringing someone on.
And this would more or less lock us into outsourcing for the next year versus maybe bringing someone in six months down the road.
Yeah, definitely like fair reactions.
Yeah.
I think the fastest way to get to an outcome here would be to go ahead and redline the PandaDocs to anywhere that you think is more mutual.
The challenge on this side is that it's a couple of things.
The first piece is we're only doing two strategy sprint kickoffs a week, and there's a ton of demand that we have against those two available kickoffs.
And the reason we're doing that is because every single client, we want to reach the highest quality, deliver ROI.
And for our business, revenue matters a lot less than churn.
And so that's obviously just words, but that is the most important thing for our business right now.
So will our team lose the hunger?
Will they lose the focus?
We need to renew you guys at the end of those 12 months.
Otherwise, we wouldn't want to bring you on as a client in the first place.
And so I think like what.

It's hard is, you know, we want the contract to be mutual, but there are several clients who, like, want to take one of these two spots each week and would do the 12-month commitment, would do, right, maybe more difficult payment terms.
I think where we can meet you in the middle is, like, quarterly payments is something that I know Marcel has said yes to before.

He has not been saying yes to monthly recently, and then if there's any, like, specific red lines you can think of, definitely propose them, and I can, you know, take them to Marcel and see if he goes for it.

I know one thing that interested him was, like, you know, you guys churned once as a client, and so if you came back to GrowthX, like, that churn would, I guess, no longer be considered churn, right, and he's, like, in these Series B conversations right now.
So, I know we're eager to work together again, it's just, the 12K was, like, one thing we did, given the prior relationship, and every client I'm talking to.
Right now is signing 30K, 15K, phase one, phase twos, and doing semi-annual or quarterly at the worst on payments.
Cool.
Yeah, that makes sense.
Cool.
All right.
I think what we need to do is just sit down with the doc a little bit and be more specific in our ask.
And then we can move from there.
Yeah, that's perfect.
And definitely feel free to pose more questions as they come up, Harrison.
I'll try and get you the exact members who will be working on your account during the strategy sprint.
And then give you a look at some of our managing editors, managing directors, and content managers so that you can see more of the background and experience they have in SEO and content marketing.
I think if you guys do want to move forward with us, we'd ideally just want to get to signature by the end of this week or early next.
It's at the absolute latest so that we could keep the 27th.
As your kickoff date.
Okay, sounds good.
Yeah, we can get that.
We can add those comments that we just mentioned.
My only other outstanding question is, like, last time we chatted, we talked about, like, the platform that you're building at the same time.
Is that something that we have access to once it's ready?
Or that would really just be used internally by the growthx team?
Yeah, 100%.
There's not going to be, like, an additional charge or anything like that.
It's basically, like, when it's ready for clients to access, you would be brought in.
Last I heard on timeline there was, like, one to two months.
I've definitely seen more come into the platform over the last couple of weeks even.
So, like, there's, like, a dashboard with different quality metrics.
You're starting to see, like, the activity.
So, things we would do in Airtable, right?
Like, pages scheduled, published, assignments that will be.
Working on, there's also this knowledge base where you can, like, upload your own sources, which is pretty cool.
Different resources that you want to also feed into the workflows in addition to the artifacts that we create.
And then there's obviously, like, all the artifacts that we would build for you to make the content more relevant.
You brought up a good point.
It reminded me of one more thing, Harrison, like, you know, growthx, the cost of, like, hiring a full SEO employee.
Absolutely.
Absolutely.
I think what you get out of it is you got the strategy, you've got the content creation, staging, and publishing daily.
And then you also have, like, the LLM visibility and analytics.
And so, like, Marcel's talked a lot about how you don't need to buy, like, Profound if you're using growthx because we're now doing all of the LLM analytics for you.
A little bit more than what you asked, but, you know, there's definitely much more coming in terms of, like, what you'll get from the platform in addition to the service that we're providing.
Thank you.
Thank
Y'all still there?
I don't know why the audio is not working, but...
Hey, Harrison's computer just roused, but we're back.
Okay, no worries.
Did I cut off in the middle of that monologue?
A little bit.
I mean, we were talking about like the...
What, it's like the AEO analytics in AeroOps and with that other tool?

Yeah, basically just that, like the cost of growthx, in addition to everything that we're doing on strategy and content creation and staging and publishing, like you also wouldn't need to go buy like Profound for 3k a month because we would do the LLM analytics and visibility for you guys as well.
And so that's like one other piece of the equation sometimes that gets overlooked.
Cool.
Okay, cool.
That makes sense.
Awesome.
Well, I'll keep my eyes out for anything that comes through in the Panda or over email and just let us kind of know where your head's at by the end of the week if that sounds good.
Okay, perfect.
All right.
Thanks for making the time.
Yeah, thanks, Chance.
Have a good weekend.
Or have a good week.
See ya.

default_summary.markdown_formatted:
## Meeting Purpose

[Review GrowthX and Attention partnership agreement and discuss SEO strategy engagement details.](https://fathom.video/share/inN5bQmr16msTyLHMPzwEmsU_WvSNBts?tab=summary&timestamp=1.0)

## Key Takeaways

  - [Attention seeks to leverage SEO for driving meetings, focusing on AI Engine Optimization (AEO) and new content creation](https://fathom.video/share/inN5bQmr16msTyLHMPzwEmsU_WvSNBts?tab=summary&timestamp=120.0)
  - [GrowthX proposes 8-week strategy sprint ($20K) + 12-month growth execution ($12K/mo), with earliest kickoff on Sept 27](https://fathom.video/share/inN5bQmr16msTyLHMPzwEmsU_WvSNBts?tab=summary&timestamp=330.0)
  - [Attention expressed concerns about 12-month commitment and semi-annual billing; GrowthX open to quarterly payments](https://fathom.video/share/inN5bQmr16msTyLHMPzwEmsU_WvSNBts?tab=summary&timestamp=687.0)
  - [GrowthX platform access included; offers LLM visibility/analytics (replacing need for tools like Profound)](https://fathom.video/share/inN5bQmr16msTyLHMPzwEmsU_WvSNBts?tab=summary&timestamp=1000.0)

## Topics

### SEO Strategy Focus

  - [Primary goal: Drive meetings through SEO](https://fathom.video/share/inN5bQmr16msTyLHMPzwEmsU_WvSNBts?tab=summary&timestamp=120.0)
  - [Key focus: AI Engine Optimization (AEO) to improve ranking and coverage with NLLMs](https://fathom.video/share/inN5bQmr16msTyLHMPzwEmsU_WvSNBts?tab=summary&timestamp=131.0)
  - [Strategy: Identify frequent queries, position for AI answer engine suggestions](https://fathom.video/share/inN5bQmr16msTyLHMPzwEmsU_WvSNBts?tab=summary&timestamp=145.0)
  - [Emphasis on new content creation over updating existing content](https://fathom.video/share/inN5bQmr16msTyLHMPzwEmsU_WvSNBts?tab=summary&timestamp=163.0)

### Engagement Structure and Pricing

  - [Strategy Sprint: 8 weeks, $20K total ($10K/mo)](https://fathom.video/share/inN5bQmr16msTyLHMPzwEmsU_WvSNBts?tab=summary&timestamp=330.0)
  - [Growth Execution: 12 months, $12K/mo ($144K total)](https://fathom.video/share/inN5bQmr16msTyLHMPzwEmsU_WvSNBts?tab=summary&timestamp=412.0)
  - [Total engagement: \~14 months, $164K](https://fathom.video/share/inN5bQmr16msTyLHMPzwEmsU_WvSNBts?tab=summary&timestamp=687.0)
  - [Earliest kickoff date: September 27, 2025](https://fathom.video/share/inN5bQmr16msTyLHMPzwEmsU_WvSNBts?tab=summary&timestamp=388.0)
  - [Content production: 5 new pieces/week during growth execution](https://fathom.video/share/inN5bQmr16msTyLHMPzwEmsU_WvSNBts?tab=summary&timestamp=363.0)
  - [10+ articles expected during strategy sprint (starting week 4-5)](https://fathom.video/share/inN5bQmr16msTyLHMPzwEmsU_WvSNBts?tab=summary&timestamp=630.0)

### Team Composition

  - [Strategy Sprint: Eric (lead), Marcel (kickoff), George (chief of staff)](https://fathom.video/share/inN5bQmr16msTyLHMPzwEmsU_WvSNBts?tab=summary&timestamp=547.0)
  - [Growth Execution: Managing Director, Managing Editor, Content Manager](https://fathom.video/share/inN5bQmr16msTyLHMPzwEmsU_WvSNBts?tab=summary&timestamp=527.0)
  - [GrowthX to provide specific team member details for Attention's review](https://fathom.video/share/inN5bQmr16msTyLHMPzwEmsU_WvSNBts?tab=summary&timestamp=933.0)

### Contract Concerns

  - [Attention views 12-month commitment as lengthy for service-based offering](https://fathom.video/share/inN5bQmr16msTyLHMPzwEmsU_WvSNBts?tab=summary&timestamp=734.0)
  - [Semi-annual upfront billing seen as favorable to GrowthX](https://fathom.video/share/inN5bQmr16msTyLHMPzwEmsU_WvSNBts?tab=summary&timestamp=687.0)
  - [GrowthX open to quarterly payments, but not monthly](https://fathom.video/share/inN5bQmr16msTyLHMPzwEmsU_WvSNBts?tab=summary&timestamp=780.0)
  - [Attention to provide specific redlines for negotiation](https://fathom.video/share/inN5bQmr16msTyLHMPzwEmsU_WvSNBts?tab=summary&timestamp=916.0)

### GrowthX Platform

  - [Client access included at no extra charge](https://fathom.video/share/inN5bQmr16msTyLHMPzwEmsU_WvSNBts?tab=summary&timestamp=1000.0)
  - [Expected availability: 1-2 months](https://fathom.video/share/inN5bQmr16msTyLHMPzwEmsU_WvSNBts?tab=summary&timestamp=1000.0)
  - [Features: Quality metrics dashboard, activity tracking, knowledge base, artifact storage](https://fathom.video/share/inN5bQmr16msTyLHMPzwEmsU_WvSNBts?tab=summary&timestamp=1008.0)
  - [Includes LLM visibility and analytics (replacing need for separate tools like Profound)](https://fathom.video/share/inN5bQmr16msTyLHMPzwEmsU_WvSNBts?tab=summary&timestamp=1059.0)

## Next Steps

  - [Attention to review contract and provide specific redlines/comments](https://fathom.video/share/inN5bQmr16msTyLHMPzwEmsU_WvSNBts?tab=summary&timestamp=916.0)
  - [GrowthX to share detailed team member information for the engagement](https://fathom.video/share/inN5bQmr16msTyLHMPzwEmsU_WvSNBts?tab=summary&timestamp=933.0)
  - [Attention to decide on moving forward by end of week/early next week](https://fathom.video/share/inN5bQmr16msTyLHMPzwEmsU_WvSNBts?tab=summary&timestamp=916.0)
  - [If proceeding, aim for contract signature by end of week to secure Sept 27 kickoff](https://fathom.video/share/inN5bQmr16msTyLHMPzwEmsU_WvSNBts?tab=summary&timestamp=958.0)

action_items.description: Review contract, add specific comments/redlines in PandaDoc. Focus on 12-month commitment, payment terms (propose quarterly), other mutual terms.
Provide details on specific team members for strategy sprint (incl. Eric) + managing editors/directors/content managers. Send LinkedIn profiles if available.
Decide w/ Harrison on moving forward w/ GrowthX. Communicate decision to Tyler by end of week/early next. Consider 27th kickoff date.
